pages.raw_text:
Tomorrow is the day you should vote.


The Press is independent newspaper it has axes
to grind but hundreds of inquiries have been received by
the Press asking which candidate for governor would be
most likely to the people The Press will give this
brief sketch of both candidates and the voter decide
for himself. ...ss.#... ..s.'...'


Albert E. Mead was nominated by republican conven-
ton that dominated by the Hill and Morgan railroads.
Representatives of these roads practically caused his
ination.


George Turner was nominated by democratic cOnven-
ton that dominated by the Rockefeller and Hartman
railroads Representatives of these roads practically
caused his nomination.


If you believe Rockefeller will do for the State of
Washington than Hill, then cast your vote for Turner


IT, the other hand you believe Hill will do for
the State than Rockefeller, vote for Mead


But do not get the foolish idea that either of these men
favor railroad commission for they do not


If Turner is elected there will be railroad commits
sion.


If Mead is elected there will be railroad commission.


1t is case of heads they win; tails you lose
